title: Custom properties and design tokens

publication.changeSummary: Technology-specific CSS: Responsive and Accessible Styling draft for Custom properties and design tokens; independent standards, browser, pedagogy, and accessibility review required. Beginner-quality contract 1.0.0 applied deterministically from existing source-linked draft facts; publication stage and reviewer state are unchanged.

sources.locator: CSS Snapshot 2024; W3C Working Group Note 2024; provisions applicable to Custom properties and design tokens
sources.claim: Custom properties store cascading token values that are substituted at computed-value time and can vary by scope or theme.

sources.locator: MDN CSS Guide; accessed 2026-07; provisions applicable to Custom properties and design tokens
sources.claim: Custom properties store cascading token values that are substituted at computed-value time and can vary by scope or theme.

canonicalExplanation.summary: Custom properties store cascading token values that are substituted at computed-value time and can vary by scope or theme.

sections.heading: Start here
sections.body: Start with this case: A --color-text token changes inside a dark theme scope and every consuming component receives the scoped value. Your goal is to create a small color, spacing and typography token layer and trace an override. Work through the example one step at a time, then use the boundary to check your answer.

sections.heading: Before you begin
sections.body: Review these declared prerequisites first: Specificity and source order; html semantics; Inheritance, initial and computed values. If one is unfamiliar, revisit it before continuing.

sections.heading: Technology rule
sections.body: Custom properties store cascading token values that are substituted at computed-value time and can vary by scope or theme. Apply this rule to the actual markup, stylesheet, JavaScript, or component state rather than memorizing its name.

sections.heading: Boundary and browser reality
sections.body: They are not preprocessor variables, can become invalid at use time, inherit by default, and need fallback design. This draft therefore rejects: Custom properties are globally fixed constants evaluated before the cascade.

includes: Define inheriting reusable values with local overrides and safe fallbacks.
Create a small color, spacing and typography token layer and trace an override.
excludes: They are not preprocessor variables, can become invalid at use time, inherit by default, and need fallback design.

outcomes: Create a small color, spacing and typography token layer and trace an override.

examples.title: Custom properties and design tokens: concrete case
examples.situation: A --color-text token changes inside a dark theme scope and every consuming component receives the scoped value.
walkthrough: Step 1 — Inspect the exact artifact and starting state: A --color-text token changes inside a dark theme scope and every consuming component receives the scoped value.
Step 2 — Use the core rule above to predict one observable result.
Step 3 — Verify the correction: Custom properties participate in the cascade and scope; define meaningful fallbacks and validate the property where var() is used.
examples.result: The result follows because Custom properties participate in the cascade and scope; define meaningful fallbacks and validate the property where var() is used.

examples.title: Custom properties and design tokens: boundary case
examples.situation: They are not preprocessor variables, can become invalid at use time, inherit by default, and need fallback design.
walkthrough: Step 1 — Start from the documented boundary: They are not preprocessor variables, can become invalid at use time, inherit by default, and need fallback design.
Step 2 — Use the core rule above to predict one observable result.
Step 3 — Reject the misconception: Custom properties are globally fixed constants evaluated before the cascade.
examples.result: The boundary requires this correction: Custom properties participate in the cascade and scope; define meaningful fallbacks and validate the property where var() is used.

misconceptions.mistakenBelief: Custom properties are globally fixed constants evaluated before the cascade.

misconceptions.diagnosticPrompt: Use a concrete CSS: Responsive and Accessible Styling artifact to explain why this belief fails: Custom properties are globally fixed constants evaluated before the cascade.

analogy.example: A layered stage design can model Custom properties and design tokens, where rules size, place, and paint existing content.
usefulFor: Building a first mental model for Custom properties and design tokens before using exact platform terms
limitations: Physical layers do not reproduce cascade algorithms, intrinsic sizing, writing modes, or user preferences.
They are not preprocessor variables, can become invalid at use time, inherit by default, and need fallback design.

recap.summary: You should now be able to create a small color, spacing and typography token layer and trace an override. Keep this boundary in view: They are not preprocessor variables, can become invalid at use time, inherit by default, and need fallback design.

Apply checkpoint 'participate in the cascade' to a new artifact.
